control_group.notes: Control participants made virtually no errors, confirming task simplicity

conformity_distribution.description: Distribution shows individual differences: 26% never conformed, 14% conformed on majority of trials

trial_position_effect.description: Conformity rates relatively stable across trial positions

group_comparison.description: Experimental group made significantly more errors than control group

conformity_rate_vs_chance.description: Mean conformity rate (37%) not significantly different from chance responding to incorrect alternatives

proportion_test.description: Proportion of conforming responses significantly greater than zero

key_findings: 37% average conformity rate on critical trials where group unanimously gave wrong answer
74% of participants conformed at least once
26% of participants never conformed (maintained independence across all critical trials)
Only 1 participant (2%) conformed on all critical trials
Control group error rate was only 1%, confirming the task was objectively easy
Individual differences were substantial: conformity rates ranged from 0% to 100%
No significant order effects: conformity remained relatively stable throughout the experiment
Participants reported awareness of group pressure but varied in their ability/willingness to resist

required_tests.description: Mean conformity rate should be between 25% and 50% (observed: 37%)

required_tests.test_name: Experimental group errors > Control group errors
required_tests.description: Experimental group should make significantly more errors than control (p < 0.05)

required_tests.test_name: Majority of participants conformed at least once
required_tests.description: At least 65% of participants should conform on at least one critical trial
Control group error rate should be below 5% (confirms task simplicity)
Standard deviation of conformity rates should be > 0.20

partial_credit_rules.description: If conformity rate is within 5% of expected range
If group difference is in correct direction but p > 0.05

replication_history.citation: Bond, R., & Smith, P. B. (1996). Culture and conformity: A meta-analysis. Psychological Bulletin, 119(1), 111-137.
replication_history.notes: Meta-analysis of 133 Asch-type studies across 17 countries. Average conformity rate: 25-35%, confirming robustness of effect.

replication_history.citation: Mori, K., & Arai, M. (2010). No need to fake it: Reproduction of the Asch experiment without confederates. International Journal of Psychology, 45(5), 390-397.
replication_history.notes: Replicated using polarizing filters instead of confederates. Female participants showed 34% conformity, similar to original.
Replicated across multiple labs with variations. Effect robust but magnitude varies by cultural context.
